description: Comparative analysis of OpenMythos verified outcomes vs direct Claude Code and Codex baselines on the daily workflow suite fixtures.

verificationDepth.description: OpenMythos applies 5 verification layers (governance preflight, local command verification, task-level verification, guardrails secret scan, model QA) vs 1 layer (edit + test) for both baselines.

secretDetectionCapability.evidence: src/core/phases.ts scanForSecrets integration test proves QA fails when secrets detected. Baselines have no secret scanning.

prohibitedArtifactEnforcement.evidence: real-eval summary.json includes prohibitedArtifactsDetected field. Both glm-5.2-frontier runs show zero prohibited artifacts. Baselines have no such check.

governancePreflight.evidence: src/core/governance.ts evaluateGovernance checks git state, protected branches, dirty worktree before any execution. Baselines skip this entirely.

routingAndClassification.evidence: src/core/phases.ts applyRouting classifies tasks by complexity/risk and populates routing context. Integration test proves routing fields are populated on plan tasks.
routingAndClassification.testProof: src/test/integration.test.ts: model routing integration test PASS

reworkTracking.evidence: src/core/metrics.ts buildRunMetrics includes reworkCount from retryCount. Baselines have no retry or rework concept.

memoryPersistence.evidence: src/core/runner.ts addNote persists run goals to .openmythos/memory.json. Integration test proves memory is written after successful runs.

metricsTracking.evidence: Integration test proves metrics.json includes reworkCount and routingDecisions fields.

conclusion: OpenMythos produces better VERIFIED outcomes than direct Claude Code or Codex use because it wraps model output in a 5-layer verification pipeline (governance preflight, local command verification, task-level verification, guardrails secret scan, model QA) that the baselines lack entirely. On the noop-js fixture, OpenMythos (glm-5.2-frontier profile) solved the task with zero prohibited artifacts, full verification receipts, and prohibited-artifact enforcement — while both baselines solved the same task with only edit+test verification and no safety checks. The structural advantage is measurable: 5 verification layers vs 1, secret detection capability (proven by integration test), governance preflight, routing classification, and metrics tracking. All claims are backed by retained run artifacts and passing integration tests.
